title: dryopteris gongylodes subsp. propinqua
intro: Dryopteris gongylodes subsp. propinqua, also known as the 'Daisy Fern' or 'Wood Fern', is a subspecies of the Dryopteris gongylodes plant, a type of deciduous to semi-evergreen fern native to subtropical and temperate regions of Asia. This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Dryopteris gongylodes subsp. propinqua are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. The plant is typically harvested for use in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
